title: Eriosorus cheilanthoides
intro: Eriosorus cheilanthoides, also known as Chilean fern, is a type of fern native to the Andes mountains in South America. This plant has been traditionally used in medicine, and its health benefits include its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may help to soothe digestive issues and reduce inflammation in the body. The bioactive constituents of Eriosorus cheilanthoides include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Eriosorus cheilanthoides is commonly prepared as a tea or tincture, and is used to treat a range of ailments, including digestive issues, skin problems, and respiratory infections.
taxonomy: Eriosorus cheilanthoides is classified within the family of plants known as Pteridaceae, commonly referred to as the fern family. This species belongs to the genus Eriosorus and is further categorized under the kingdom Plantae. It is also classified under the division Pteridophyta, which includes all types of ferns. The classification of Eriosorus cheilanthoides is further detailed with its order Polypodiales and the class Polypodiopsida. This classification helps in identifying the plant and its evolutionary relationships with other fern species.
